chunk_summary: This section discusses integrating clinic care with public nutrition programs in India to address stunting and chronic undernutrition, emphasizing program linkages, growth monitoring, and structured documentation for continuity of care.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of public nutrition programs like Mission POSHAN 2.0 and PM POSHAN in addressing stunting and undernutrition.
Learn how to integrate clinic-based care with public platforms for continuity of care.
Develop skills to document stunting phenotypes, determinant barriers, and program actions for follow-up.
Recognize the importance of growth monitoring, anemia management, and deworming in pediatric and adolescent care.
key_facts: Mission POSHAN 2.0 supports supplementary nutrition and growth monitoring for children under 6 years.
The Poshan Tracker is a governance tool for monitoring growth and ensuring accurate measurements.
PM POSHAN provides school meals to address nutrition for school-age children, with barriers like stigma and attendance needing attention.
Anemia platforms target both school-going and out-of-school adolescents, including married adolescent girls.
National Deworming Day schedules and vitamin A rounds are critical for comprehensive care.
Structured documentation includes stunting phenotype, determinant barriers, and program actions to ensure auditable follow-up.
reference_quote: The Poshan Tracker is used for growth monitoring governance; clinicians can add value by validating abnormal measurements in clinic and reinforcing correct technique.
document_key_topics: Stunting and chronic undernutrition in children and adolescents
Integration of clinic care with public nutrition programs
Structured documentation for continuity of care
